title: Background Tasks
description: AIFunctionFactory tools start Task.Run work; results inject as <task_notification> into the next agent turn.

steps.content: Start two background tasks: one to build the project and one to run tests.
steps.annotation: The user requests work that may take time. The agent uses start_task tools.

steps.content: I'll start both tasks in the background.
steps.annotation: The agent decides to call start_task for each operation.
AIFunctionFactory.Create registers start_task. It launches Task.Run and returns immediately with a task ID.
The tool returns a task ID immediately — the agent loop is not blocked.
A second background task starts concurrently.
Both tasks now run in parallel via Task.Run.
The demo waits for tasks to finish. In production, the agent would continue chatting.

steps.content:
<task_notification id="bg_a1b2">Completed: dotnet build</task_notification>
<task_notification id="bg_c3d4">Completed: dotnet test</task_notification>
steps.annotation: Completed results are collected and injected as a ChatMessage(ChatRole.User, ...) into the next RunAsync call.

steps.content: Both tasks completed: the build succeeded and all tests passed.
steps.annotation: The agent consumes the <task_notification> as fresh context and summarizes the results.
